semantic_journey: Arabic halqa = ring, circle. Latin alcohol derives from Arabic al-kuhl = kohl, powdered antimony eye cosmetic. Two completely different Arabic words conflated: halqa (ring) vs al-kuhl (kohl). Alcohol entered Latin directly from al-kuhl, not from halqa. Misidentification of the Arabic source root.
key_insight: Two different Arabic words conflated: halqa (ring) has no connection to al-kuhl (kohl), the actual Arabic source of Latin alcohol.

semantic_journey: Arabic difda = frog. Latin paludifer = marsh-making. The proposed link: frogs live in marshes so frog-name connects to marsh-maker. This is a 3-step chain: frog name -> frog habitat -> habitat descriptor. No cognate relationship possible.
key_insight: Arabic frog-name vs Latin marsh-making compound: ecological proximity is not etymology.

semantic_journey: Arabic naqara = to peck, strike; Form II naqqara = to investigate, scrutinize, probe. Latin insequor = to follow after, pursue. Step 1: pecking/probing -> directed pursuit. Plausible but weak, as insequor is transparently in-sequor meaning to follow after, a native Latin compound with its own etymology.
key_insight: Arabic naqara covers physical pecking and investigative probing; Latin insequor covers pursuit, but Latin has its own transparent etymology.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-arak = arak tree (Salvadora persica), the toothbrush tree native to Arabia. Latin larix = larch tree (Larix decidua), native to Alpine Europe. Both are tree names for completely different trees from different geographic regions. Sharing the category of tree does not make them cognates.
key_insight: Two unrelated tree names from different geographic and cultural contexts: sharing the category of tree is not a semantic connection.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-tahabras = swaggering strut, walking with exaggerated elevated gait. Latin colobathra = stilts, an elevated walking device. Step 1: manner of elevated walking -> tool that produces elevated walking. A 1-step specialization from behavioral description to the device producing that behavior.
key_insight: Arabic elevated-prancing-gait maps onto Latin stilts via plausible 1-step specialization.

semantic_journey: Arabic baba/bab-bab = to buzz, to make a buzzing or humming sound, onomatopoeic. Latin bombio = to buzz, to hum. Direct onomatopoeic preservation. Both encode the same sound: the labial-stop/labial-stop pattern b-b mimicking buzzing. The consonant skeleton b-b maps to b-mb in Latin with nasal insertion.
key_insight: Arabic bab-bab (buzz) and Latin bombio (buzz) share direct onomatopoeic preservation, b-b -> b-mb being a regular Latin nasal insertion pattern.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-halaka from root halak = intense blackness, deep dark coloration. Latin/Medieval Latin alcohol derives from Arabic al-kuhl = kohl, the powdered black antimony eye cosmetic. Step 1: conceptual blackness -> the canonical black cosmetic (kohl). Halaka (blackness) -> al-kuhl (the black thing) is a coherent 1-step concept-to-substance path.
key_insight: Arabic halaka (intense blackness) connects to al-kuhl (kohl = the black substance), the actual etymon of Latin alcohol, via 1-step path through the blackness concept.

semantic_journey: Arabic naqar in Form II (naqqara) = to investigate, examine, scrutinize, probe. Latin anquiro = to search carefully, investigate thoroughly, examine judicially. Step 1: physical pecking/probing -> investigative probing (Form II shift complete in Arabic). The Latin preserves this investigative sense directly. Stronger than LAT_0874 because anquiro specifically means careful thorough investigation.
key_insight: Arabic naqqara (to investigate/probe deeply) and Latin anquiro (to investigate thoroughly) share the concept of directed intensive probing-investigation, a 1-step semantic path.
